output.context: CSS-only refactoring. Modal close button styles moved from resources/css/screen/swiper.css to blocks/TestimonialsCarousel/TestimonialsCarousel.css. @apply Tailwind directives converted to plain CSS. No visual changes expected.
output.compositionNotes: Overall layout, structure, proportions, visual weight, and card positioning all match the Figma reference at 1440px desktop. Dark background, lime/yellow-green cards, heading typography, navigation arrows, and progress bar all render correctly.

1440.notes: 3 visible cards + partial 4th card peek. Dark background, bold heading, description text, accent line, navigation arrows, and card content all render identically across all 3 browsers. No visual regressions.

1024.notes: Layout adapts to show 3 cards. Typography scales correctly. Card spacing and proportions maintained. Cross-browser rendering consistent. No visual regressions.

768.notes: 2 cards visible per view. Responsive layout correct. Card content readable. Navigation elements properly positioned. Cross-browser consistent. No visual regressions.

375.notes: Single card layout. Cards scroll as expected for mobile. Typography readable. Navigation controls appropriately sized. Cross-browser consistent. No visual regressions.

output.crossBrowserConsistency: PASS - All 12 screenshots (3 browsers x 4 widths) show identical visual output with no browser-specific rendering differences.

refactorVerification.change: modal-close-btn styles moved from swiper.css (@apply Tailwind) to TestimonialsCarousel.css (plain CSS)
refactorVerification.notes: The modal close button is a modal-triggered element not visible in default page state. The carousel block itself (cards, navigation, layout, typography, colors) renders identically to the Figma reference across all breakpoints and browsers. The refactoring has not introduced any visual regression.
